article_title: DECISION Trial Misses Primary Endpoint as Pooled Meta-Analysis of 9,013 Patients Finds Digitalis Cuts Heart Failure Events 15 Percent
summary: Submission approvable with 3 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: DECISION Trial Misses Primary Endpoint as Pooled Meta-Analysis of 9,013 Patients Finds Digitalis Cuts Heart Failure Events 15 Percent
content_preview.summary: Low-dose digoxin failed its primary endpoint in 1,001 Dutch heart-failure patients, but a JAMA meta-analysis of DECISION, DIGIT-HF and the 1997 DIG trial published the same day finds digitalis cuts the combined risk of cardiovascular death or first worsening event by 15 percent.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Dutch-led DECISION trial of low-dose digoxin in chronic heart failure missed its primary endpoint when results were presented on May 10 at the Heart Failure Association of the European Society of Cardiology's annual congress in Barcelona, according to a [press release from the European Society of Cardiology](https://www.escardio.org/news/press/press-releases/new-positive-data-on-old-drugs-digitalis-reduces-heart-failure-events/). On the same day, a study-level meta-analysis comb...

recommendations: Consider adding escardio.org, jamanetwork.com, and acc.org to config/source_allowlist.txt — canonical primary cardiology sources.

editor_notes.content_quality: Exceptionally rigorous 1,220-word medical News piece on the May 10 ESC Heart Failure 2026 readouts. Per-trial breakdown of DECISION, DIGIT-HF, the pooled JAMA meta-analysis, and the withdrawal-analysis subgroup is handled with full numerical fidelity (n, age, sex, AF prevalence, follow-up duration, hazard ratios with 95% CIs, p-values).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read 4 fetched snapshots. source-0 ESC press release (15.9k chars): all the central JAMA meta-analysis numbers are verbatim — 'HR 0.85; 95% CI 0.80 to 0.90; p<0.001' for composite, 'HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.69 to 0.81; p<0.001' for worsening HF — even though JAMA itself was bot-blocked. All Veldhuisen quotes verbatim ('Digoxin is the oldest drug in cardiovascular medicine, but there has been uncertainty about its value in HF(m)rEF management' and 'In patients with HF(m)rEF, low-dose digitalis glycosides seem to be an effective additional medical treatment option, which are cheap, safe and easy to use'). DECISION numbers (n=1,001, 43 sites Netherlands, 36.5 months follow-up, 73 years mean age, rate ratio 0.81 CI 0.61-1.07 p=0.133, NCT03783429) all verbatim. van der Meer withdrawal RR 7.37 CI 1.56-34.88 p=0.012 verbatim. Kevin Damman as lead author confirmed. source-2 PubMed (14.4k chars): DECISION design paper confirms the serum-level target (0.5-0.9 ng/ml). source-3 ACC (8k chars): DIGIT-HF 39.5% vs 44.1% endpoint event rates, Bavendiek principal-investigator role, serious-adverse-event rates 4.7% vs 2.8% all verbatim. source-4 Medical Xpress via archive.org fallback (14k chars): DIGIT-HF context, Bauersachs + Bavendiek at Hannover Medical School, foxglove-derived characterization. Bot-blocked sources: JAMA (source-1) — full meta-analysis numbers corroborated in ESC press release; Eur Heart J Suppl (source-5) — DIGIT-HF specifics corroborated in ACC and Medical Xpress.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics verified across the four fetched snapshots. The JAMA-attributed numbers are independently confirmed in the ESC press release; the Eur Heart J Suppl-attributed DIGIT-HF HR (0.82, CI 0.69-0.98, p=0.03) is independently confirmed in the ACC snapshot.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and clinically precise. Correctly distinguishes the null DECISION primary result from the positive meta-analysis. 'What we do not know' section appropriately flags the absence of a mortality signal and the open question about interaction with SGLT2 inhibitors.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the May 10 ESC heart-failure readouts.
concerns: Two cited URLs (jamanetwork.com and academic.oup.com/eurheartjsupp) returned HTTP 403 at fetch and were not archived locally. The corrections record below documents the transparency gap for readers; the substantive content from both is corroborated in the four fetched snapshots.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Editorially clean and exceptionally rigorous; corrections record documents the two un-archivable primary sources.
